records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
IAST ARCHIVE — E_4_Notes
Term / Field
Explanation
KHAṆḌA 3 — Dūṣaṇa Pañjī
Document
VOL_III.docx — Dūṣaṇa Pañjī (dūṣaṇa pañjī) — 
Ṭebulakā entries 1–97
Nature
A genealogical register of lineage pollutions / 
prohibited alliances (dūṣaṇa = blemish/pollution). 
Records kulas with inter-caste marriages, devadāsī 
connections, nimna-kula (degraded lineage), niṣiddha-
kula (prohibited lineage), kocarāja-patnī (wives of cow-
herder kings), antaḥjātīya (inter-caste) unions.
Structure
Each numbered entry (1–97) records: (1) village list 
across columns; (2) personal names in those villages 
across 2–5 rows; (3) footer line = progenitor reference 
(sutā/sutra + ref number like 178/1) + special_status 
note
Entry numbering
1–97 (ṭebulakā 1–97 as stated in header). Some 
numbers missing = merged with prior entry (e.g. 5 
absent, entry 4 has two sub-blocks)
Column structure
Tab-separated: each tab = one village column. Variable 
number of columns per entry (6–12). Village names in 
row 1, personal names in rows 2+, aligned positionally.
Progenitor_Ref_IAST
Cross-reference number (e.g. 178/1, 208/2) linking to 
the Upa-Tīpa Pañjī. Format: [register_no]/[entry_no]
Special statuses
nimna-kula = degraded/lower gotra; niṣiddha-kula = 
prohibited lineage (marriages forbidden); antaḥjātīya = 
inter-caste union; devadāsī = temple 
dancer/courtesan; kocarāja-patnī = wife of 
cowherd/low-caste king
(ajñāta)
ajñāta = unknown/unidentified person in that village 
column
IAST
ISO 15919 — same standard as Khaṇḍa 2. IPA→IAST 
conversion applied to all fields.
Header fill
#1F3864 white bold Arial 11pt
Batch
1 (COMPLETE — all 97 entries) — single batch 
sufficient for this khaṇḍa
Parsed by

All ancestors sorted by marriage count; title 
annotations

rikta
